mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rate-hiking periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth outpaces inflation, corporate earnings expand without margin compression, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as investors price in sustainable profit growth.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, which translates into positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.02 during expansion phases, or positive returns drop below 55% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation persistently above 3.0% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing recession probabilities and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20d returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market expansion that supports household income and consumer spending, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p>0.05) across multiple rolling windows, or positive return frequency falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, reducing recession fears and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive Rank IC > 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or equity returns turn negative in >55% of co-occurring periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment during a late-cycle phase signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering sharp negative equity repricing over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with >60% accuracy during late-cycle periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.
